title: pteris podophylla var. setifera
intro: Pteris podophylla var. setifera, also known as the Setifera Brake Fern, is a type of fern native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its lacy fronds and distinctive setae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The Setifera Brake Fern is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used in the form of a paste or powder in traditional medicine.
